title: hieracium viscosum subsp. viscosum
intro: Hieracium viscosum subsp. viscosum is a plant species in the sunflower family, known for its sticky, hairy leaves and stems, and is also referred to as sticky hawk's beard. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Hieracium viscosum subsp. viscosum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have also been used in salves and ointments for topical application.
